title: centaurea scabiosa var. alpestris
intro: Centaurea scabiosa var. alpestris, also known as Alpine Knapweed, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its purple to pinkish flowers and a preference for dry, rocky areas. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a digestive aid and anti-inflammatory, which is attributed to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of Centaurea scabiosa var. alpestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried herb for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
